seatPosition.name: Seat Position Visualizer
seatPosition.description: See how sliding or tilting your kart seat shifts front/rear weight and CoG height — with a calibration mode for corner scales.

seat.diagramAria: Side view of the kart showing seat position and centre of mass
seat.diagramLegend: Gray ghost = zero point · crosshair = combined CoG · dot = tilt anchor
seat.cogPosition: {{x}} mm fwd of rear axle · {{z}} mm high
seat.sensNaive: Naive "whole driver moves" estimate: {{value}}%/in — feet-on-pedals model is the lower number.
seat.cornerNote: Rigid-frame approximation — karts corner on frame jacking, so treat as a relative indicator.
seat.kartDriverNote: The baseline is your kart at the zero point — most sprint setups target 43% front (some run 40). Corner scales beat guesses: see Calibration.
seat.legCouplingNote: How much of the seat's movement the leg CoM follows (feet stay on the pedals). 0.4 is a sane default — calibrate it on scales.
seat.calibrationIntro: Default numbers are ±20% per driver. Two scale sessions anchor the model to your kart: weigh at the zero point, then slide the seat a known amount and re-weigh to fit the leg coupling.
seat.calStep1: 1 · Corner weights at the zero point (driver seated)
seat.calStep2: 2 · Slide the seat a known amount and re-weigh the front pair

landing.tileDescription: Trackside calculators and utilities — no datalog needed.
landing.noTools: No tools are available in this build.

laptimer.name: Lap Timer
laptimer.description: Turn your phone's GPS into a live lap timer — delta, lap times and sector splits, saved as a log you can review.
laptimer.endDialogBody: This stops logging and saves the session to your files. You can't add more to it afterward.
laptimer.savedHint: Find it in your saved files to review like any log.
laptimer.noDataBody: You need to be moving (above 5 mph) for a session to record.
laptimer.noLaps: No laps yet — complete a lap to see times here.
laptimer.speedometerMode: Speedometer mode — no tracks found nearby
laptimer.farHint: Data being logged for post-race analysis. Create a track here later to get lap times.
laptimer.waitingHint: Drive above 5 mph to start logging.
